dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 8938 IH: Biotechnology Workforce Alignment Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. McCormick (for himself and Mr. Khanna) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology

form.official-title: To direct the Director of the National Science Foundation to carry out activities to support biotechnology workforce pathways and alignment with Federal research investments, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Biotechnology Workforce Alignment Act of 2026.

section.header: NSF support for biotechnology workforce pathways and alignment with Federal research investments

paragraph.text: The Director, in coordination with the heads of relevant Federal agencies, shall carry out activities to support the integration of Federal biotechnology research investments with workforce development activities.
In carrying out paragraph (1), the Director shall identify high-demand biotechnology workforce areas, including the following:

subparagraph.text: Omics, such as genomics and proteomics.
Such other areas as the Director determines appropriate.

subsection.text: In carrying out this section, the Director shall carry out the following:

paragraph.text: Support the development of a workforce framework, including relating to workforce categories, work roles, and competency areas, for biotechnology.

Disseminate information on, and support educational and career pathways with multiple entry and exit points for, students and early-career researchers leading to credentials and degrees in biotechnology workforce areas.
Promote participation in internships, apprenticeships, cooperative education programs, and other hands-on training opportunities.

Support partnerships among institutions of higher education (including community colleges and minority-serving institutions), Federal laboratories, nonprofit organizations, and private sector entities.
Disseminate best practices for workforce development activities.

subsection.text: In carrying out this section, the Director shall, to the extent practicable, carry out the following:

paragraph.text: Coordinate with private sector entities engaged in biotechnology research, development, or manufacturing.
Support model frameworks for industry-academic partnerships.
Encourage alignment between workforce training programs and evolving industry needs.

subsection.text: The Director shall develop metrics to assess the following:

paragraph.text: Workforce gaps in biotechnology careers.
Barriers to entry into biotechnology careers.
The effectiveness of coordination and partnerships under this section.

subsection.text: The Director shall, to the extent practicable, carry out this section through integration with existing National Science Foundation programs and activities.

Not later than two years after the date of the enactment of this Act and biennially thereafter for six years, the Director shall submit to the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate a report that includes the following:

paragraph.text: An assessment of workforce gaps in biotechnology careers.
A description of activities carried out under this section.
An evaluation of outcomes associated with workforce development activities.
An assessment of the position of the United States biotechnology workforce in maintaining global leadership in biotechnology relative to key competitor and near-peer countries.
Recommendations for actions to strengthen Federal coordination relating to biotechnology initiatives and workforce development activities.

The term biotechnology means the use of biological systems, organisms, or derivatives thereof to develop or manufacture products, including biomanufacturing, bioprocess engineering, synthetic biology, genomics, proteomics, and computational biology applications.
The term Director means the Director of the National Science Foundation.

The term workforce development activities includes internships, apprenticeships, cooperative education, experiential learning, skills-based training, and related activities that prepare individuals for employment in biotechnology careers.
